description: A program run with an already-aborted CancellationSignal (harness executeOptions.signal, issue #195 — a plain `{ aborted: true }` object, the only shape JSON fixture data and execute() itself both need) halts before its first statement runs with ol-limit/cancelled and no events at all. This is the third and last of the three ol-limit params (`instruction-budget`, `recursion-depth`, `cancelled`), closing the corpus gap noted at M1 close: ol-limit had zero execute-mode fixtures despite being fully implemented and unit-tested (spec/execution-model.md:551-557, spec/error-model.md:119). A fixture cannot express a signal that flips mid-run (that needs a stateful JS getter, not JSON), so the mid-run cancellation scenario stays proven only by packages/runtime/src/execution-budget.test.mjs's direct unit test.
